item.title: Why 2026 politicians are stuck in 2016
item.description: Contemporary problems require modern solutions not defunct ideas.

item.title: A dark analogy for the war in Iran
item.description: From a “realist” perspective, Russia’s invasion of Ukraine offers some unsettling parallels.

item.title: The drone swarm in Louisiana is a warning about the future of war
Ohio’s drag on free speech
item.description: The state’s restrictions are clearly an attempt to chill protected expression.

item.title: Who knows you best?
item.description: Edith Pritchett cartoon on how well algorithms know people.

item.title: Yet another step toward state capitalism
item.description: Competing with China by imitating its methods won’t work.

item.title: Another government shutdown that won’t end
item.description: Politicians talk a big game about reform but have accomplished nothing by withholding funding.

item.title: A free-speech farce in Finland
item.description: It’s not a crime to publish unpopular religious views.

item.title: Ask this question on a first date — or brace for a nasty surprise
item.description: Hot topics, AI dream girls and the Kennedy Center, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: We run small businesses. This is what Trump’s tariffs did to us.
item.description: Layoffs, refunds, Graham Platner and the gold Trump coin, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: Reopen the Strait of Hormuz? There’s one big problem.
item.description: A brief history of sea mines, the weapon likely standing in President Donald Trump’s way.

item.title: Cartoon by Rob Rogers
item.description: Rob Rogers cartoon on verdict in Meta, YouTube, social media addiction trial.

item.title: What happened at LaGuardia Airport?
item.description: Making sense of this week’s crash — and how to reform air-traffic control.

item.title: In Israel, wartime reality doesn’t match what you see on the internet
item.description: Both the liberal internationalist left and the isolationist right are being duped by online propaganda.

item.title: Local control in jeopardy if Virginia mandates collective bargaining
item.description: Spanberger can show seriousness on affordability by vetoing a bill that would lead to higher property taxes.
